friction_items.description: close_reading.yaml step `declare_step_contract` references `tool_humanities_apparatus_audit` as if it exists (only hedged with 'if available; planned, see docs/ROADMAP.md'). The tool is not in _HANDLERS, not in TOOL_DEFINITIONS, not in the humanities pack. AI clients reading the protocol will call it and get a 'tool not found' from the dispatcher — opaque error, no auto-fallback to the generic completeness gate even though the protocol is designed around that fallback.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either ship tool_humanities_apparatus_audit in v2.0.0 (it is small: presence-check for line anchors + tradition header + counter-instance + secondary-criticism ledger against quality_bar) OR rewrite the close_reading step to stop naming a phantom tool. Pick one for v2 — the current state is a v1.9.3 IOU that has already shipped twice.

friction_items.description: Humanities pack router_entries.py uses sub_intent='method_pick' for 5 protocols (close_reading, distant_reading, hermeneutic_method, digital_humanities_workflow, scholarly_edition's siblings). Core _router_index.yaml hierarchy under methodology defines the sub_intent as 'pick' (and many others — causal/ml/qualitative/etc). The drift means tool_route's L2 ambiguity resolution and ask_user prompts can't match these pack entries against the canonical hierarchy. Preflight does not currently catch this taxonomy mismatch.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Rename humanities pack sub_intent 'method_pick' → 'pick' to match the core hierarchy. Add a preflight wiring check that every pack router_entry's (intent_class, sub_intent) tuple exists in the core hierarchy block of _router_index.yaml, fail loud if not.

friction_items.description: 204 unique tool names exposed to the AI client. Figure-audit cluster alone has 5 variants (tool_audit_figure, _coverage, _full, _interactivity, _quality — one already aliased to _full). For a humanities close-reading scenario that emits zero figures, all 5 are noise in tool listings and semantic search. Wider counts: 23 audit_* tools, 9 mem_* tools (4 aliased), 5 sys_protocol_* (could be one with operation kwarg), 4 sys_checkpoint_* (same).
friction_items.suggested_fix: Phase 9 consolidation: collapse figure-audit cluster 5→1 (operation= coverage|interactivity|quality|full), protocol cluster 5→1, checkpoint cluster 3→1. Mark non-applicable clusters per-pack so tool listings filter to humanities-relevant tools when domain=humanities (the active_tools filter exists; extend it to suppress figure-* tools for humanities mode).

friction_items.description: Maintainer wants to know: which tools never get called, which deprecated aliases are still hot, which protocols route to dead ends, which pack registers a phantom tool. reliability.jsonl exists locally but there is no maintainer-facing aggregate. tool_reliability_report exists per-project but no cross-project view, no 'unused tool' report. sys_active_tools shows the current snapshot but no historical decay.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add tool_reliability_unused_tools (scan _HANDLERS vs reliability.jsonl in a project; emit list of never-called handlers since N days). Add a CI-time check that walks every protocol's text + decomposition for `tool_<name>` references and fails if the name is not in _HANDLERS or _ALIASES. That would have caught the phantom apparatus_audit ref.

friction_items.description: close_reading.yaml description block is 50 lines of literary-theory name-dropping (I.A. Richards, New Critics, Empson, de Man, Greenblatt, McGann, Sedgwick, Spivak, Said, Iser, Fish, Auerbach, Curtius, Jakobson). Beautiful for a humanities reader; opaque for a non-humanities maintainer trying to grep what the protocol actually DOES. Token_estimate 1500 is dominated by exposition, not actionable steps.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Move the theoretical lineage to a `background:` block (loaded only with format=full), keep description to a 3-5 sentence operational summary. Bring token_estimate for default load (summary) down to ~600. Same treatment for hermeneutic_method and humanities_essay_structure.

friction_items.description: humanities_essay_structure declares `shortcut_tool: tool_humanities_essay_scaffold` at the bottom but the close_reading router entry has NO shortcut_tool, so tool_route can't short-circuit to a single call when the user just wants the scaffolded annotation file. distant_reading and digital_humanities_workflow also have no shortcut_tool despite both having obvious one-call entry points.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Audit every pack router entry: if a protocol's first step is `tool_X (shortcut)` in its description, promote that to shortcut_tool in the router entry so tool_route can offer the one-call path. Add preflight check: protocols with shortcut_tool: must have a matching router_entries shortcut_tool.

friction_items.description: tool_audit_step_completeness humanities-mode blocker text is good (lists alternatives clearly) but the recurring_blockers messages in lines 304-329 use phrases like 'deferred-pattern audit gate blocks final assembly' without telling the maintainer WHICH gate, WHICH config knob, or HOW to override. The `override_completeness_gate=true with rationale` hint appears in one branch but not in the gate that fires first.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Standardize all audit blocker messages to include: (1) the gate name (e.g. `gate: literature_deferred_recurring`), (2) the config flag to suppress (e.g. `set audit.suppress_gate=literature_deferred_recurring in researcher_config.yaml`), (3) the override tool + required rationale. Make this a docstring contract on `_blockers.append` callsites.

friction_items.description: close_reading.next_protocol = humanities/citation/citation_chains. citation_chains.next_protocol = humanities/output/scholarly_edition (or essay). The chain from close_reading to humanities_essay_structure (which is what the scenario asks for) is NOT direct — researcher has to know to override next_protocol. humanities_essay_structure.trigger DOES say it 'fires from humanities/textual/close_reading.next_protocol once apparatus+critical ledger are sufficient' but close_reading's next_protocol points elsewhere. Self-inconsistency.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Make close_reading.next_protocol point to humanities_essay_structure (or add a branch: if humanities_essay_intent then essay, else citation_chains). Add preflight rule: if protocol A's docstring says it fires from B.next_protocol, then B.next_protocol MUST be A (transitively if branched).

friction_items.description: research_os_humanities.__init__.__version__ = '1.9.3', package version is 1.11.0. Pack version stale by two MINOR bumps. close_reading.yaml version: '1.9.3' too. The semantic embeddings npz file in protocols/ is regenerated per release but pack-side protocols have not been re-stamped — preflight will not catch this since version: is free-form in pack protocols.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Phase 11 release-prep: bump every pack __version__ + every pack protocol version: to '2.0.0' when bumping the core. Add a preflight wiring check that pack protocol versions match the pack __version__.

friction_items.description: humanities/textual/close_reading decomposition has exactly one entry: `{protocol: humanities/textual/close_reading, purpose: Walk the annotate → claim → ground loop}` — it routes to itself with no tool steps. Same for distant_reading and hermeneutic_method. The whole point of decomposition is to give the AI a tool plan; a single self-reference is degenerate.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either populate decomposition with the actual tool sequence (e.g. close_reading → sys_file_write inputs/textual/passages/, mem_log tradition, sys_file_write workspace/close_readings/, mem_log apparatus, tool_humanities_apparatus_audit) OR drop the decomposition key entirely and let tool_route fall back to the protocol load.

convoluted_tools: tool_audit_figure_full vs tool_audit_figure vs tool_audit_figure_coverage vs tool_audit_figure_quality vs tool_audit_figure_interactivity — 5 variants for one concern, 1 already aliased
tool_audit_step_completeness (overloaded: figure gate + literature gate + provenance sidecar gate + pipeline.yaml gate — single blocker message conflates them)
tool_route vs tool_semantic_route vs tool_quick_route vs sys_semantic_tool_search — four routing entry points, unclear precedence
sys_protocol_get + sys_protocol_list + sys_protocol_next + sys_protocol_validate + sys_protocol_history + sys_protocol_log + tool_protocols_list (7 protocol-meta tools)

tool_dashboard_create + tool_dashboard_test_generate + tool_dashboard_test_run + tool_dashboard_reviewer_sim + tool_dashboard_story_edit/_generate/_quality_bar (irrelevant clutter in humanities scenarios)
confusing_protocols: humanities/textual/close_reading — 50-line theory-of-literary-criticism preamble buries the 7 actionable steps; token_estimate 1500 mostly exposition

humanities/synthesis/humanities_essay_structure — references humanities_essay.typ template + synthesis_paper handoff + venue layer + citation styles all in one description; AI has to chase three dependencies to act
humanities/method/hermeneutic_method — sub_intent 'method_pick' but it is itself a method, not a method-picker (taxonomy collision)
humanities/archival/source_provenance vs humanities/citation/citation_chains — both scaffold quotation chain markdown; unclear which fires when; both have tool_humanities_citation_chain as shortcut
recommendations: Ship tool_humanities_apparatus_audit in v2.0.0 (it is the single highest-impact fix — un-blocks the close_reading protocol's promise) OR remove the reference. Stop shipping protocols with phantom-tool IOUs.
Add preflight wiring check: every `tool_<name>` mention in any protocol YAML must resolve to a real handler or alias. Walk the YAML text + decomposition + shortcut_tool fields. Fail loud.
Add preflight wiring check: every pack router_entries (intent_class, sub_intent) tuple must exist in core _router_index.yaml hierarchy. Catches the humanities `method_pick` drift.

Phase 9 tool consolidation: collapse the figure-audit cluster (5→1), protocol-meta cluster (7→2: sys_protocol + sys_protocol_history), checkpoint cluster (3→1). Should drop ~15 tools without losing capability.

Pack-aware tool filtering in sys_active_tools: when domain=humanities, hide figure_*/dashboard_*/slurm_*/r_exec/notebook_*/sensitivity_* (the 30+ STEM-only tools). Cuts cognitive surface roughly in half for humanities sessions.

Standardize audit blocker messages to (gate_name, override_flag, override_tool, rationale_required). Currently each audit message invents its own override hint or omits it. Make it a docstring contract.

Trim protocol descriptions: humanities pack averages 40 lines of literary-theory preamble per protocol. Move to optional `background:` block; default-load summary should be ≤10 lines, ≤500 tokens. Halves token_estimate.
Fix close_reading.next_protocol to chain to humanities_essay_structure for the close-reading→essay path (or branch). Currently the documented trigger story is self-inconsistent.
Bump every pack __version__ + every pack protocol version: in lockstep with the core 2.0.0 bump in release-prep phase. Add preflight check that pack protocols match pack version.
